question: Would the yearly precipitation on Snowdon submerge an upright bowling pin?
context: Snowdown gets about 200 inches of precipitation a year
A standard bowling pin is one foot, three inches tall
response:
<reasoning>
Snowdown receives approximately 200 inches of precipitation annually.
A standard bowling pin is 15 inches tall.
200 inches of precipitation is greater than 15 inches.
The yearly precipitation on Snowdon would exceed the height of an upright bowling pin.
<answer> Yes
